summary: Explorer can show the full path in the title bar. Microsoft Open Specifications documents the exact FullPath user preference under Explorer\CabinetState, and the 25H2 default hive exports the same value as 0. The current app writes the documented enabled state under that same Explorer preference surface.

setting.name: Show Full Path in Explorer
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer can show the full folder path instead of a shorter title. Some advanced users prefer that when they navigate deep directories.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it on when they want more exact path context while browsing folders.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Showing the full path can be more informative for deep folder navigation, but it also makes the title bar busier.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Open Specifications documents FullPath under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\CabinetState and states that 1 enables showing the full path in Explorer title bars while 0 disables it. The 25H2 default hive exports FullPath = 0 in the Explorer\CabinetState block, which corroborates the current default preference state on that snapshot.

allowed_values.label: Explorer default behavior
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer keeps its current baseline because no explicit FullPath preference is set by this record.

allowed_values.label: Do not show full path
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer does not display the full path in the title bar.

allowed_values.label: Show full path
allowed_values.meaning: Explorer displays the full path in the title bar.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes FullPath = 1, which matches the documented Explorer user-preference surface.

windows_defaults.label: 25H2 default Explorer preference
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current-user Explorer CabinetState settings on the 25H2 default hive snapshot

states.rationale: HKCU25H2.reg exports FullPath = 0 in the default Explorer\CabinetState block.

windows_defaults.notes: This is current 25H2 default-hive corroboration rather than a guarantee for every historical build.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who do not need exact path context in the title bar

states.rationale: This matches the documented disabled state and the 25H2 default-hive export.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You prefer the standard Explorer title bar behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Explorer to always show the exact folder path.
tradeoffs: You do not get exact path context in the title bar.

recommended_profiles.label: Show full path
intended_for: Users who work in deep folder trees
avoid_for: Users who prefer simpler Explorer chrome

states.rationale: Use the documented Explorer preference to show the full path.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want exact path context in Explorer windows.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer shorter, cleaner title bars.
tradeoffs: Explorer shows the full path in the title bar.
The title bar becomes more verbose.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: GlobalFolderOptions
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the FullPath Explorer preference under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\CabinetState and states that 1 enables showing the full path while 0 disables it.

evidence.title: 25H2 default hive corroboration for FullPath
evidence.summary: The 25H2 default hive exports FullPath = 0 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\CabinetState.

evidence.title: Repo source note for explorer.show-full-path
evidence.summary: The repo source note links the FullPath registry write to upstream nohuto research and marks the tweak as repo-backed.

decision.why: Microsoft Open Specifications documents the exact FullPath Explorer preference under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\CabinetState and states that 1 enables showing the full path while 0 disables it. The 25H2 default hive corroborates the current disabled default state, and the app writes the documented enabled state.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Path: Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\CabinetState; Value Name: FullPath; Description: Displays the full path to the folder. MUST be 1 to enable and 0 to disable.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft Open Specifications publishes the exact Explorer CabinetState path, FullPath value name, and 0/1 semantics. The app writes the documented enabled state under the same user-preference surface.
